summary: Comprehensive security sweep for multi-tenant SaaS platform - ALL TESTS PASS. Verified tenant isolation, IDOR prevention, Admin tab visibility, and partner_code display. 22 backend tests passed, 3 skipped (no test data available). UI tests confirm customer cannot see Admin tab while admin can.

orders.py (lines 238-245): subscription_id lookup includes tenant filter - VERIFIED WORKING
subscriptions.py (lines 302-308): customer_id update validates tenant scope - VERIFIED WORKING
subscriptions.py (line 372): cancel webhook uses tenant-scoped customer lookup - VERIFIED WORKING
bank_transactions.py (lines 66-71): linked_order_id validation with tenant filter - VERIFIED WORKING
tenant.py: Platform admin (role=platform_admin, tenant_id=null) can see ALL data by design - this is expected behavior

seed_data_creation: Created Tenant B test data: 1 customer (tbcustomer@tenantb.local), 1 order, 1 subscription

tenant_a_platform_admin.note: Platform admin - sees ALL tenants by design

tenant_b_admin.note: Tenant-scoped admin - only sees Tenant B data

context_for_next_testing_agent: All security features verified working. Key insight: admin@automateaccounts.local is platform_admin (tenant_id=null) which correctly sees all data - this is by design. For IDOR testing, use tenant-scoped admins like adminb@tenantb.local. Tenant B test data was created for testing cross-tenant isolation.

admin_tab_visibility.customer_test: Customer sees only Store, Articles - NO Admin tab
admin_tab_visibility.admin_test: Admin sees Store, Articles, AND Admin tab

idor_prevention.direct_resource_access: 404 returned when Tenant B tries to access Tenant A resources

tenant_isolation.orders: Tenant B cannot see Tenant A orders
tenant_isolation.customers: Tenant B cannot see Tenant A customers
tenant_isolation.subscriptions: Tenant B cannot see Tenant A subscriptions
